questions.stem:
—Have you seen the new movie *Nezha*?
—Yes, I ______ it with my classmates last Friday.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。答句中有明确的时间状语"last Friday"（上周五），表示过去的具体时间点，应使用一般过去时。A选项saw是see的过去式，符合语境。B选项have seen是现在完成时，不能与具体过去时间连用；C选项see是原形，不符合时态；D选项had seen是过去完成时，用于表示"过去的过去"，此处无此含义。解题技巧：看到ago, yesterday, last week等过去时间标志词，用一般过去时；看到already, yet, since, for等，用现在完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is playing basketball over there is my cousin Bosco.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词是"The boy"（指人），且关系代词在从句中作主语（is playing的主语），因此用who。B选项which指物；C选项whom指人但作宾语；D选项whose表示"谁的"，在从句中作定语。解题技巧：指人作主语用who，指人作宾语用whom/who，指物用which/that，表示所属关系用whose。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The old bridge near our school ______ in 1990.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。主语"The old bridge"与动词"build"之间是被动关系（桥被建造），且时间状语"in 1990"表示过去，因此用一般过去时的被动语态was/were + 过去分词。A选项builds是一般现在时主动；B选项built是过去式或过去分词，但缺少be动词，不能构成被动；D选项is built是一般现在时被动，与1990时间不符。解题口诀：被动语态be done，时态看be，过去用was/were。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: Tom runs ______ than any other student in his class. He is the fastest runner in the school sports meeting.
questions.explanation: 本题考查比较级的用法。句中有"than"（比），提示使用比较级。A选项fast是原级；C和D是最高级。B选项faster是fast的比较级，符合"than any other"结构（比班上其他任何学生都快）。注意："than any other + 单数名词"是一种特殊的比较级结构，表示最高级含义，但形式上用比较级。解题技巧：看到than选比较级（-er或more），看到in/of范围选最高级。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The ______ of the mountain is covered with snow all year round, while the foot is green and warm.
questions.explanation: 本题考查通过上下文猜测词义。根据后半句"while the foot is green and warm"（而山脚是绿色温暖的）以及"covered with snow"（被雪覆盖）和"all year round"（终年），可推断此处指"山顶"。A选项foot指山脚，与后半句重复；C选项side（侧面）通常不会终年积雪；D选项bottom（底部）与foot同义。解题技巧：利用while（然而）表示的对比关系，以及常识（高处寒冷积雪）进行推断。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We are all ______ to seeing our favorite science teacher at the new term opening ceremony.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。look forward to doing sth. 表示"期待做某事"，符合语境（期待见到老师）。A选项look for表示"寻找"；B选项look after表示"照顾"；D选项look at表示"看"。解题技巧：记住常见短语含义，forward有"向前"之意，look forward即"向前看"引申为"期待"，后接to + 名词/动名词。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: Bosco worked very ______ on his science project and won the first prize in the competition.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词性转换。此处修饰动词"worked"（工作/努力），需用副词形式。A选项success是名词（成功）；B选项successful是形容词（成功的）；C选项successfully是副词（成功地）；D选项succeed是动词（成功）。解题技巧：修饰动词用副词（通常以-ly结尾），修饰名词用形容词，作主语/宾语用名词。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 根据上下文语境，后文提到"students joined the project actively"（学生积极加入项目）以及后文描述的种种活动，可知是"许多"学生参加。Many修饰可数名词复数students。Few表示"很少"，与积极语境不符；No表示"没有"，与文意相反；Little修饰不可数名词，不能修饰students。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green project last month. The goal was to make our campus more environmentally friendly. (1)______ students joined the project actively. At first, we discussed what we could do in our daily school life. Some suggested saving water, (2)______ others wanted to recycle paper and plastic bottles.

We decided to start with small steps. Every morning, volunteers stand (3)______ the classroom doors to remind classmates to turn off the lights when leaving. We also set up special bins for different (4)______ of waste. Students quickly got used to separating rubbish into recyclable and non-recyclable categories.

After two weeks, we noticed big changes. The amount of rubbish (5)______ significantly, and our school garden looked cleaner than ever before. Teachers and students all felt proud of our efforts to protect the environment.

questions.explanation: 考查逻辑关系连接词。前文"Some suggested..."（一些人建议...），后文"others wanted..."（另一些人想要...），表示两种不同观点的对比。While在此处表示"而、然而"，连接并列句表示对比。And表示并列；but表示转折（语气较强）；or表示选择。Some...while others...是固定搭配，表示"一些...而另一些..."。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green project last month. The goal was to make our campus more environmentally friendly. (1)______ students joined the project actively. At first, we discussed what we could do in our daily school life. Some suggested saving water, (2)______ others wanted to recycle paper and plastic bottles.

We decided to start with small steps. Every morning, volunteers stand (3)______ the classroom doors to remind classmates to turn off the lights when leaving. We also set up special bins for different (4)______ of waste. Students quickly got used to separating rubbish into recyclable and non-recyclable categories.

After two weeks, we noticed big changes. The amount of rubbish (5)______ significantly, and our school garden looked cleaner than ever before. Teachers and students all felt proud of our efforts to protect the environment.

questions.explanation: 考查介词辨析。Near the classroom doors表示"在教室门附近/旁边"，符合语境（志愿者站在门旁提醒同学）。By也表示"在...旁边"，但通常指紧贴、紧挨；at表示在具体的点（如at the door指在门口正中间，不太符合实际）；under表示"在...下面"，逻辑不通。Near表示在附近区域，最符合实际场景。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green project last month. The goal was to make our campus more environmentally friendly. (1)______ students joined the project actively. At first, we discussed what we could do in our daily school life. Some suggested saving water, (2)______ others wanted to recycle paper and plastic bottles.

We decided to start with small steps. Every morning, volunteers stand (3)______ the classroom doors to remind classmates to turn off the lights when leaving. We also set up special bins for different (4)______ of waste. Students quickly got used to separating rubbish into recyclable and non-recyclable categories.

After two weeks, we noticed big changes. The amount of rubbish (5)______ significantly, and our school garden looked cleaner than ever before. Teachers and students all felt proud of our efforts to protect the environment.

questions.explanation: 考查固定搭配。Different kinds of表示"不同种类的"，后文提到"recyclable and non-recyclable categories"（可回收和不可回收类别），提示是按种类分类。Colors（颜色）、shapes（形状）、sizes（尺寸）虽然也可以分类垃圾，但与后文的"categories"（类别）以及环保语境中的垃圾分类（按材料种类）不符。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green project last month. The goal was to make our campus more environmentally friendly. (1)______ students joined the project actively. At first, we discussed what we could do in our daily school life. Some suggested saving water, (2)______ others wanted to recycle paper and plastic bottles.

We decided to start with small steps. Every morning, volunteers stand (3)______ the classroom doors to remind classmates to turn off the lights when leaving. We also set up special bins for different (4)______ of waste. Students quickly got used to separating rubbish into recyclable and non-recyclable categories.

After two weeks, we noticed big changes. The amount of rubbish (5)______ significantly, and our school garden looked cleaner than ever before. Teachers and students all felt proud of our efforts to protect the environment.

questions.explanation: 考查词汇辨析及语境理解。根据前文"big changes"以及后文"cleaner than ever before"（比以前干净），可知垃圾量"减少"了。Reduced表示"减少"；increased（增加）、grew（增长）、added（增加）都与语境相反。解题技巧：通过上下文情感线索（积极变化、感到自豪）推断应是正面结果，即垃圾减少。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green project last month. The goal was to make our campus more environmentally friendly. (1)______ students joined the project actively. At first, we discussed what we could do in our daily school life. Some suggested saving water, (2)______ others wanted to recycle paper and plastic bottles.

We decided to start with small steps. Every morning, volunteers stand (3)______ the classroom doors to remind classmates to turn off the lights when leaving. We also set up special bins for different (4)______ of waste. Students quickly got used to separating rubbish into recyclable and non-recyclable categories.

After two weeks, we noticed big changes. The amount of rubbish (5)______ significantly, and our school garden looked cleaner than ever before. Teachers and students all felt proud of our efforts to protect the environment.

questions.stem: How many hours do teenagers spend on screens daily according to the study mentioned in the passage?
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据文章第一段第二句"A recent study shows that the average teen spends about 7 hours daily on phones and computers"（最近一项研究显示，普通青少年每天花在手机和电脑上的时间约为7小时），可知答案是7小时。解题技巧：细节题要准确定位关键词（study, hours, daily），在原文中找到原句或同义句，注意数字信息。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers spend more time on screens than ever before. A recent study shows that the average teen spends about 7 hours daily on phones and computers. This excessive use has raised concerns among parents and educators.

Dr. Li, a researcher from Shenzhen University, explains that too much screen time affects sleep quality. "The blue light from screens tricks our brains into thinking it's still daytime," he says. This makes it hard for teens to fall asleep at night, which leads to poor performance in school the next day.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and online courses can help students learn better if used properly. The key is balance. Experts recommend the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Many schools in Shenzhen are now encouraging outdoor activities. Students are asked to put away their phones during breaks and play sports instead. This has helped improve both their eyesight and social skills. As one student Bosco said, "Playing basketball with friends is much more fun than staring at a screen."

questions.stem: The underlined word "excessive" in Paragraph 1 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文，前文提到青少年每天花7小时在屏幕上，后文说"has raised concerns"（引发担忧），可推断excessive表示"过度的、过多的"。A选项necessary（必要的）、C选项proper（适当的）与担忧语境不符；D选项limited（有限的）与7小时这一较长时长矛盾。B选项too much（太多）符合语境。解题技巧：利用因果关系（花7小时→引发担忧）和情感色彩（concerns为负面）推断词义。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers spend more time on screens than ever before. A recent study shows that the average teen spends about 7 hours daily on phones and computers. This excessive use has raised concerns among parents and educators.

Dr. Li, a researcher from Shenzhen University, explains that too much screen time affects sleep quality. "The blue light from screens tricks our brains into thinking it's still daytime," he says. This makes it hard for teens to fall asleep at night, which leads to poor performance in school the next day.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and online courses can help students learn better if used properly. The key is balance. Experts recommend the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Many schools in Shenzhen are now encouraging outdoor activities. Students are asked to put away their phones during breaks and play sports instead. This has helped improve both their eyesight and social skills. As one student Bosco said, "Playing basketball with friends is much more fun than staring at a screen."

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Li's words in Paragraph 2?
options: A. Teenagers should study harder at night.
B. Blue light is good for brain development.
C. Using screens before bed may cause sleep problems.
D. Students perform better when they sleep less.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第二段Dr. Li的解释："The blue light from screens tricks our brains into thinking it's still daytime... This makes it hard for teens to fall asleep at night"（屏幕的蓝光欺骗大脑，让它以为还是白天...这使得青少年晚上难以入睡），可推断睡前使用屏幕可能导致睡眠问题。A选项文中未提及；B选项与原文（蓝光欺骗大脑）含义相反；D选项与原文"poor performance"（表现差）相反。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能过度推断，注意排除与原文矛盾的选项。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers spend more time on screens than ever before. A recent study shows that the average teen spends about 7 hours daily on phones and computers. This excessive use has raised concerns among parents and educators.

Dr. Li, a researcher from Shenzhen University, explains that too much screen time affects sleep quality. "The blue light from screens tricks our brains into thinking it's still daytime," he says. This makes it hard for teens to fall asleep at night, which leads to poor performance in school the next day.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and online courses can help students learn better if used properly. The key is balance. Experts recommend the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Many schools in Shenzhen are now encouraging outdoor activities. Students are asked to put away their phones during breaks and play sports instead. This has helped improve both their eyesight and social skills. As one student Bosco said, "Playing basketball with friends is much more fun than staring at a screen."

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The Harm of Digital Devices
B. How to Use Educational Apps
C. Balancing Screen Time and Healthy Life
D. Outdoor Activities in Shenzhen Schools
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章第一段讲过度使用屏幕的问题，第二段讲对睡眠的影响，第三段讲适度使用的益处和20-20-20规则，第四段讲学校鼓励户外活动。全文围绕"平衡屏幕时间与健康生活"展开。A选项只涵盖第一、二段（危害），片面；B选项只涉及第三段部分内容；D选项只涉及第四段。C选项"Balancing Screen Time and Healthy Life"（平衡屏幕时间与健康生活）涵盖了问题、危害和解决方案，最全面。解题技巧：最佳标题要覆盖全文主要内容，既不能太宽泛，也不能太片面，通常包含文章讨论的核心矛盾。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers spend more time on screens than ever before. A recent study shows that the average teen spends about 7 hours daily on phones and computers. This excessive use has raised concerns among parents and educators.

Dr. Li, a researcher from Shenzhen University, explains that too much screen time affects sleep quality. "The blue light from screens tricks our brains into thinking it's still daytime," he says. This makes it hard for teens to fall asleep at night, which leads to poor performance in school the next day.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and online courses can help students learn better if used properly. The key is balance. Experts recommend the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Many schools in Shenzhen are now encouraging outdoor activities. Students are asked to put away their phones during breaks and play sports instead. This has helped improve both their eyesight and social skills. As one student Bosco said, "Playing basketball with friends is much more fun than staring at a screen."

options: A. However, with good planning, you can do both well.
B. For example, reading or playing sports can be good choices.
C. As a result, you won't waste time on unnecessary things.
D. In addition, it helps you develop good time management skills.
E. Then, you should list your hobbies in order of importance.
questions.explanation: 考查段落衔接与逻辑关系。前句说"很多学生发现很难平衡学习和爱好"，后句说"这里有一些有用的建议"，中间需要过渡句。A选项"然而，通过好的规划，你能同时做好这两件事"承上启下，既承认困难（转折），又引出下文的建议。解题技巧：关注前后句逻辑关系，前句讲问题/困难，后句讲解决方法，中间常用however/with good planning等过渡。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance their studies and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they have too much homework but still want to enjoy their free time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a schedule. Write down your study time and hobby time clearly. (18)______ You will know exactly when to study and when to relax.

Second, focus on your tasks. When you study, put away your phone and avoid distractions. (19)______ This helps you finish homework faster and have more time for hobbies.

Finally, choose hobbies that truly help you relax. (20)______ These activities can refresh your mind and improve your study efficiency.

options: A. However, with good planning, you can do both well.
B. For example, reading or playing sports can be good choices.
C. As a result, you won't waste time on unnecessary things.
D. In addition, it helps you develop good time management skills.
E. Then, you should list your hobbies in order of importance.
questions.explanation: 考查递进关系。前句讲"制定时间表，清楚写下学习时间和爱好时间"，后句讲"你会确切知道何时学习何时放松"。D选项"此外，它帮助你培养良好的时间管理技能"中的it指代前文的making a schedule，且in addition表示递进，补充说明制定时间表的好处。A已用过；B是举例；C是结果；E是步骤。此处需要递进补充。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance their studies and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they have too much homework but still want to enjoy their free time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a schedule. Write down your study time and hobby time clearly. (18)______ You will know exactly when to study and when to relax.

Second, focus on your tasks. When you study, put away your phone and avoid distractions. (19)______ This helps you finish homework faster and have more time for hobbies.

Finally, choose hobbies that truly help you relax. (20)______ These activities can refresh your mind and improve your study efficiency.

options: A. However, with good planning, you can do both well.
B. For example, reading or playing sports can be good choices.
C. As a result, you won't waste time on unnecessary things.
D. In addition, it helps you develop good time management skills.
E. Then, you should list your hobbies in order of importance.
questions.explanation: 考查因果关系。前句讲"学习时把手机收起来，避免干扰"，后句讲"这帮助你更快完成作业，有更多时间给爱好"。C选项"结果，你不会在不必要的事情上浪费时间"表示结果，连接前后因果关系。As a result（因此、结果）是因果连接词，符合"收手机→不浪费时间→更快完成作业"的逻辑链。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance their studies and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they have too much homework but still want to enjoy their free time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a schedule. Write down your study time and hobby time clearly. (18)______ You will know exactly when to study and when to relax.

Second, focus on your tasks. When you study, put away your phone and avoid distractions. (19)______ This helps you finish homework faster and have more time for hobbies.

Finally, choose hobbies that truly help you relax. (20)______ These activities can refresh your mind and improve your study efficiency.

options: A. However, with good planning, you can do both well.
B. For example, reading or playing sports can be good choices.
C. As a result, you won't waste time on unnecessary things.
D. In addition, it helps you develop good time management skills.
E. Then, you should list your hobbies in order of importance.
questions.explanation: 考查举例关系。前句讲"选择真正能帮助你放松的爱好"，后句讲"这些活动能提神并提高学习效率"。B选项"例如，阅读或运动可以是好的选择"是对前句"hobbies"的举例说明，且后句的"These activities"（这些活动）指代的就是reading or playing sports。For example是举例信号词。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance their studies and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they have too much homework but still want to enjoy their free time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a schedule. Write down your study time and hobby time clearly. (18)______ You will know exactly when to study and when to relax.

Second, focus on your tasks. When you study, put away your phone and avoid distractions. (19)______ This helps you finish homework faster and have more time for hobbies.

Finally, choose hobbies that truly help you relax. (20)______ These activities can refresh your mind and improve your study efficiency.
